free_text: New measurements of excitation functions of 186W(p,x)
nuclear reactions up to 65 MeV. Production of a
178W/178mTa generator
Natural high purity metallic W foils (21.3 um)
One stack
containing W, Al, GaNi, Au, Cu layers was irradiated
with a proton beam of 65 MeV and 35 nA for 1 h.
Activity of the foils was measured in four series
to follow the decay of the reaction products.
No chemical separation was applied.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the semi
empirical formula of Andersen and Ziegler.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.5MeV which increased up to +-1.5MeV in the last
target foil of the stack.
No details are given on the partial error
components
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
